summary: Scorching Fire & One More Saturday Night opener, tight second‑set flow
blurb: The show kicks off with a blistering One More Saturday Night that segues into a fiery Fire On The Mountain. Highlights include Bird Song, Terrapin Station and a magical second‑set transition through Cryptical Envelopment to The Other One. Jerry’s guitar shines while the band delivers high‑energy playing throughout.
review:
Ventura County Fairgrounds on July 13 1985 delivered one of those quintessential mid‑’80s Dead nights that still feels fresh today. The first set bursts out with a rare, high‑octane One More Saturday Night opener before launching into a scorching Fire On The Mountain; the band never lets up, weaving in Dupree's Diamond Blues, a rollicking Big River and an exuberant Bird Song that rolls straight into The Music Never Stopped.  The second set builds a seamless arc – from Touch of Grey through Terrapin Station, Drums, Space and The Wheel – into a beautifully eerie Cryptical Envelopment before exploding on The Other One and winding down with Comes A Time and Sugar Magnolia.  Across the board the recordings capture Jerry’s deep, rich tone (he even “shreds” Fire), Phil’s popping bass foundation (despite occasional static), and crisp, high‑energy drumming from Mickey and Bill.  While a few audience tapes note wind and minor sloppiness, the crystal‑clear soundboard sources preserve every nuance, making this show a must‑listen for anyone chasing that summer ’85 highlight vibe.

For listeners seeking the definitive audio experience, the George SBD (gd85-07-13.sbd.georges.372.sbefail.shnf) stands out as the cleanest, most complete capture – crystal‑clear, with only negligible bass static and no dropouts.  It showcases the band at peak energy while preserving the subtle dynamics of the second‑set journey, solidifying this night as a standout entry in the Dead’s mid‑’80s canon.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings (3) describe the performance in glowing terms – 'crystal‑clear', 'scorching Fire', 'high‑energy', 'deep, rich guitar' – while only one recording offers a negative note limited to pop clicks on The Other One and two are mixed mainly about recording quirks.  No consistent criticism of playing is present, so the sentiment is strongly positive, warranting a substantial +1.4 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with excellent fidelity, minimal static and full set completeness; best represents the band's high‑energy performance.

key_highlights: Scorching Fire On The Mountain that showcases Garcia’s shredding guitar.
Rare high‑octane One More Saturday Night opener that launches the night.
Magical second‑set flow through Cryptical Envelopment into a soaring The Other One.
Bird Song delivers a tight, melodic jam that bridges first and second sets.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers deep, rich tones and an outright shredding Fire; his guitar work is energetic throughout, with only a brief rushed moment on Touch of Grey.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a solid, popping bass foundation; occasional static noted but overall strong support for the high‑energy set.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s keyboards sit slightly lower in the mix than typical ’80s boards yet remain present and supportive of the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Cymbals sparkle, adding to the high‑energy feel; drumming is tight and drives the set forward.
band_performance.Billy: Drums are crisp and powerful, especially on the first‑set rockers and the second‑set transitions.
